transcript: Until now, the only way to renew a U.S. passport was through the mail by going to the post office. In August 2022, the State Department launched a pilot program that allowed a limited number of people to renew their passports online in August and then again in October. I did not share information about this online program when it launched because it was brand new and I didn't want all of you to be guinea pigs. With any new program, it takes a few months of running to smooth out all the kinks. So now that they've run it for a few months, let's talk about how it works. Currently, it opens up for a limited amount of time every month. Once they get a certain number of applications, they close it out for a month and then open it again the following month. Once they've made all the adjustments they need to make sure it runs smoothly, I expect that this online program will be made permanent and available to the entire public later this year. At the time that this video was filmed, here's who qualifies to renew their passport online. If this changes, I will publish an updated video, but if you don't see one then this information still holds true So let's go to the State Department website to see what criteria you need to meet in order to apply for your passport online So this is the home page and it has changed a little bit since some of my last videos So that's also why I wanted to show it to you. You simply click on get a US passport and now Since you're renewing you'll go to I already have a passport and you'll see that there are two options whereas before there used to be one i'm an adult renewing by mail which is what we've historically done and i'm an adult renewing online and so now when you go here you'll see that it gives you some information and it tells you that it's temporarily open and it's limited release and all the things I mentioned earlier now how do I know if I can renew online it's laid out here pretty well so I'm just going to run through this but if you want more time to read this just head on over to the site like I've shown you but basically your most recent passport was valid for 10 years and you're over the age of 25 in order to be able to do this. Your recent passport was issued over nine years, but less than 15 years from the date you plan to submit your application. And it's okay if your passport is expired. Now you're not changing your name, gender, date of birth, or place of birth. If you need to change any of these things you'll have to apply through mail you're not traveling internationally for at least six weeks from the date you will submit your applications so while the processing times are the same i do think that if you're in a bit more of a rush then i would definitely apply vm mail because there are a few different options to make it come a little quicker And then again you're applying for a regular passport which is most of you right if you have a special passport then you cannot do this you live in the US this is very important you cannot do this online if you're living in a foreign country you do have your passport with you it's not damaged and you've never reported it lost or stolen and you do keep your recent with you. It's not damaged, and you've never reported it lost or stolen. And you do keep your recent passport. You don't have to mail it to them when you apply online. And like I mentioned, the payment methods have to be the ones I described. You can't do money order or check or anything like that. And then, very important, you need to be able to upload a digital passport photo. And it's very easy to do you can do the same thing as before you can go to a cvs walgreens write it wherever you can get a passport photo but you tell them that you need a digital photo and they will put it on a usb drive for you which you can then upload to your application and be aware that when you apply online even though you're not sending your passport the second you submit your application that passport is no longer valid for international travel okay so these are the criteria as of the time of the filming of this video that you need to meet now if you meet these criteria and want to proceed this is what you have to do it's pretty clear on the website you create and activate your my travel gov account it's very easy you just follow this link and do it to activate the account they will send you a verification email you must click on it and then you wait up to 24 hours for them to register your account and after 24 hours you can log in and click on the renew password button to start your application and again if you can't do it during this limited release they will open it up again the following month and then they give you your support numbers these are the same support numbers they've always have, as well as an email address. And that is really it. And there are a few more frequently answered questions here. But let's get into a little bit more information. If you've waited 24 hours and still do not see the option to renew your password online, they might have closed the portal for the month and received the max number of applications that they're able to accept. If that happens, I recommend just filing your renewal the old-fashioned way through the mail either way the processing times are the same for more information on how to do that check out this video eventually they will open this up to everyone and everyone will be able to see this renew your passport online option as long as you meet the criteria. Now, can you renew a passport card online? Yes, you can renew both a passport and passport card online, but only if you already have one. If you only have a passport, which you want to renew, but you also want to get a passport card for the first time, you cannot do that online online. For that you'll have to apply via mail which I explain in this video. As for payment it costs the same which is $130 for the passport book and $30 for the passport card plus any optional fees like expediting for $60 and if you'd like one to two day delivery then an additional $18 to $19 for that. When you are filling out your application online, it will take you to a page where you can see all these options and select whatever you'd like. It will have to be with a credit card or debit card or ACH, which directly transfers the funds from your bank account to theirs. For that, you'll need your bank routing number as well as the account number and just a reminder you only need a passport card if you intend on driving over the border either from Canada to the U.S. and vice versa or from the U.S. to Mexico and back if you're mostly interested in air travel you don't need a passport card. Everyone wants to know how long it will take for their passport to get renewed so I want to show you the best way to figure this out regardless of when you watch this video. The easiest thing is to go back to the State Department website. I'm on the home page click on US passports and they now actually have a tab called get my passport fast that gives you all your different options now for renewal if you just expand this here where it says expedited renewal currently whether you're renewing or applying for a passport for the first time the expedited option that you're paying the $60 for is the next three to five weeks now this changes routinely during the pandemic it was much longer and this is just a guideline but in order to take advantage of this timeline i would recommend you mail the form instead of doing it online because online it says you know if you're expecting it in less than six weeks we can't promise that right so if you do want your passport in the next three to five weeks this is the best way and i was just speaking to a couple this past week that both applied for their passports and one person received it after one week and the other is going on four weeks and is still waiting so they will get it to you within that window but even if you applied on the exact same day from the exact same post office you may not get him at the exact same time so just Thank you. So they will get it to you within that window, but even if you apply it on the exact same day from the exact same post office, you may not get it at the exact same time. So just keep that in mind. Now, I just want to point out that they've said that the processing times are the same between applying via mail and online. But they also say that if you need your passport and are traveling in the next six weeks to apply via mail and not online so that is somewhat of a contradictory statement and in the future this will not be so once the program is completely rolled out and is an option available to everybody the timelines will be exactly the same the same ranges across both online as well as via mail so this is just in this interim phase while they're sorting out the kinks so if you're not in a rush I would do it online but if you plan travel if you have travel planned in the next six weeks then I would recommend doing it via mail one other thing to know about the online application is that once you start it you have 30 days to complete it so you can save and close out and come back to it if you aren't able to complete it in one sitting if you have any other questions about passports in general I have several videos about applying for passports for the first time renewals and soon I'll be publishing one about emergency travel if you found this video helpful be sure to subscribe to the channel share it with
